source_note: GRFC Sep Update reports Gaza at 100% high acute food insecurity and over half a million facing starvation in July 2025.
IPC reports half the population 24.6M in high acute food insecurity and famine detected/projected in several areas.
Reported 7.8M facing high acute food insecurity and famine risk in 2026.
GRFC Sep Update lists Yemen among worst affected by share and among top five by number in 2025.
GRFC Sep Update reports Haiti among five highest shares of high acute food insecurity.
GRFC Sep Update lists Nigeria among five worst-hit by total people experiencing high acute food insecurity in 2025.
GRFC Sep Update lists DRC among five worst-hit by total people in high acute food insecurity in 2025.
GRFC Sep Update lists Myanmar among worst five by total people experiencing high acute food insecurity in 2025.
GRFC brief notes Afghanistan among countries with at least one-third of population in high acute food insecurity.
GRFC brief notes Ethiopia among major food crises in 2024; 2025 data gap noted in September update.
Public reporting citing GRFC lists Bangladesh among top five by number in 2024.
GRFC brief notes Syrian Arab Republic among countries with 9M to 22M people affected and at least one-third of population affected.
GRFC brief places Pakistan among countries with 9M to 22M people facing high acute food insecurity.
GRFC Sep Update notes deterioration was projected for Somalia due to rainfall outlook.
GRFC Sep Update lists Mali among countries/territories with IPC/CH Phase 5 populations.
GRFC brief notes Zambia among countries with at least one-third of population in high acute food insecurity.
GRFC brief notes Central African Republic among countries with at least one-third of population in high acute food insecurity.
GRFC brief notes Lebanon experienced escalating conflict/insecurity affecting food security.
GRFC Sep Update notes projected deterioration for Kenya due to forecast poor rainfall though rains later improved.
Hunger Hotspots reporting identified Mozambique among countries where acute food insecurity may worsen.
